notes: Verified via WebSearch 2026-07-19: Remembrance Sunday = second Sunday of November, fixed as official rule in 1956. Mathematically always equivalent to 'Sunday nearest to 11 November' since the 2nd Sunday always falls between the 8th-14th (Wikipedia).

core.type='floating', month:11, weekday:0 (Sunday, matching JS Date.getDay() convention confirmed via nthWeekdayOfMonth in src/lib/holidays-engine.js), nth:2 — zero new engine work, same floating-type pattern already proven on summer-bank-holiday-uk (weekday:1/nth:-1).
Main ceremony at the Cenotaph, Whitehall, London, with royal family attendance and two-minute silence — standard well-documented civic fact.
Poppy symbol traced to the 1920s, inspired by the WWI poem 'In Flanders Fields' — real cultural depth not found in thin competitor coverage.

Explicitly noted this is NOT itself a bank/statutory holiday (unlike new-years-day-uk, boxing-day-uk etc. on this site) — a voluntary national day of remembrance, stated clearly in content to avoid misleading readers.
Single-country event (_countryCode: gb), aliasSlugs: [] to avoid base_country_leakage.
Content is civic/historical — no direct religious content, despite some ceremonies taking place at places of worship.
